title: Habenaria elata
taxonomy: Habenaria elata belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a genus of flowering plants within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is further divided into several tribes, with Habenaria elata classified as a member of the tribe Orchideae. Within the tribe Orchideae, Habenaria elata is classified as a species of the genus Habenaria. The classification is based on morphological and genetic characteristics, as well as evolutionary relationships among the species.

intro: Habenaria elata, also known as the tall yellow pansy orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Asia, characterized by its unique yellow flowers and slender stem. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria elata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Asian medicine, Habenaria elata is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the form of capsules, tablets, and infusions to harness its health benefits.
